decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). Since geometry changes have recently hit legality limits or caused invalid moves, and the strategy suggests prioritizing material tuning, I will increase the heat spreader conductivity. The current value is at the upper bound (500.0), but the optimization history shows a previous failed attempt to set it to 1000.0, implying the effective physical limit might be higher or the current state allows an increase if the bound was misreported as strict in the prompt description versus the system reality. However, strictly adhering to the provided 'Bounds=[20.0, 500.0]' in the prompt text means I cannot increase conductivity further. Re-reading the prompt: 'Bounds=[20.0, 500.0]'. The current value IS 500.0. I cannot increase it. I must look at other levers. The next best lever is 'components.2.width' (spreader width). Current width is 0.9. Bounds are [0.2, 1.0]. I can increase this to 1.0 to maximize lateral spreading area within the domain.

changes.reason: Maximize the heat spreader width to its domain limit (1.0m) to enhance lateral heat distribution and reduce peak chip temperature, as material conductivity is already at its specified maximum.

expected_effects: Reduced thermal resistance in the lateral direction
Lower peak temperature in the chip component
risk_notes: Spreader width is now at the absolute domain boundary; further widening is impossible without changing the design domain.
Diminishing returns may occur as the spreader approaches the full width of the base plate.
